twitter_thread: 8BitDo's wireless controller sales jumped 50% YoY to 2.5M units, with a 25% profit margin.
Their N64-themed Ultimate 2 Wireless Controller sold out in 2 weeks, with 80% of buyers ordering custom designs.
The Ultimate 2's custom receiver reduces input lag by 30%, outperforming competitors.
8BitDo now owns 30% of the retro gaming market, with partnerships with 20 influencers driving sales.
Their customers buy an average of 3 accessories, including $50 controller skins and $20 charging docks.
8BitDo's average revenue per user is $200, 2x the industry average.
What's the most you've spent on a wireless controller? Was it worth it? #wirelessgaming #retrogaming
pack.linkedin: 8BitDo's 2.5M wireless controller sales last year came with a 25% profit margin, a rare feat in the electronics industry. Their focus on retro gaming enthusiasts and partnerships with 20 influencers helped them gain 30% market share. But what drives their customers to spend an average of $200 per user, 2x the industry average? Is it the 30% reduction in input lag or the ability to customize controllers with $50 skins? I'd love to hear about your experiences with wireless controllers.
tiktok_hooks: 8BitDo made $125M selling wireless controllers last year, here's their secret
I benchmarked 8BitDo's Ultimate 2 controller and the results were shocking
You won't believe what 8BitDo's customers are willing to pay for customization
pack.reddit_headline: Is 8BitDo's $70 Ultimate 2 Wireless Controller worth the premium over cheaper alternatives?
